page: Amazon Web Services r4.xlarge is a memory-optimized fourth-generation instance. It is powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor running at 2.3 GHz, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance provides 4 virtual CPUs derived from 2 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled, alongside a dedicated CPU allocation. It features 30.0 GB of system memory, yielding approximately 7.62 GB of RAM per core. There is no local storage bundled with this instance, and network connectivity is rated at a baseline of 1 Gbps. The virtualization is managed via the Xen hypervisor.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the r4.xlarge is positioned on the lower end of modern compute capabilities. Single-core and multi-core CPU tests, such as stress-ng and Geekbench, place the instance in the bottom 10% of performance. Memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks also falls into the bottom 10%, though small-block cached read bandwidth performs slightly better. The Passmark memory score achieves an average rating, reflecting a stable memory subsystem despite the older processor architecture. Database operations and Redis SET performance are measured as weak to poor, reflecting the legacy nature of the underlying hardware.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r4.xlarge offers a high memory-to-vCPU density without the premium associated with newer generation hardware. However, the tradeoff is significantly lower compute throughput and memory bandwidth compared to modern alternatives. This instance is best suited for memory-intensive legacy workloads, moderate database caching, or enterprise applications that require a larger memory footprint but do not demand high CPU performance or high-speed local storage.
description: Amazon Web Services r4.xlarge is a memory-optimized, fourth-generation instance designed for workloads requiring a high memory-to-vCPU ratio. Powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor running at 2.3 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, it provides 4 vCPUs and 30.0 GB of RAM. The instance operates on the Xen hypervisor and does not include local storage, relying instead on network-attached storage with a 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data indicates that while its memory subsystem achieves average performance scores, its overall compute and memory bandwidth capabilities are weak to poor compared to modern standards. This makes the r4.xlarge a qualitatively cost-effective option for legacy memory-bound applications, basic database operations, and caching layers that require substantial memory capacity but have modest processing requirements.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r4.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 30 GB of RAM, and an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor, suitable for legacy memory-bound workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r4.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 4 vCPUs, 30 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, running on the Xen hypervisor.
tagline: A memory-optimized legacy instance offering high RAM density for memory-bound workloads with modest compute requirements.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor with 4 vCPUs and 30.0 GB RAM
High memory-to-vCPU ratio of 7.5 GB per core
No local storage with a 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Average memory subsystem performance on Passmark benchmarks
Suited for legacy memory-intensive applications and basic database caching
